regimeNotes: NEUTRAL regime maintained 2026-07-08. Equities: SPY closed July 7 at $747.71 (S&P 500 index 7,503.85, -0.45% on the day) after a record-close July 6 (+0.72%, Dow 53,055.91) -- the pullback was driven by a chip-sector-specific rout (Samsung earnings disappointment plus reports China's DeepSeek is developing its own AI chip triggered a >4.5% drop in semiconductor names), not a broad macro deterioration; NVDA itself still closed +0.71% at $196.93, diverging from the broader chip selloff. Rates: 10Y yield rose to 4.50% (2-week high) as an LNG-carrier incident in the Strait of Hormuz pushed oil higher and revived inflation concern; September rate-hike odds rose to ~58% (from ~56%) -- a mildly hawkish/tightening signal, not a cut. DXY ~100.85, flat. Crypto: BTC $63,325 (+0.87% 24h), ETH $1,779.79 (+0.89%), SOL $81.41 (+1.62%); total crypto mcap ~$2.271T (+0.43% 24h), BTC dominance 55.80% (essentially flat). Fear & Greed 27 'Fear' (up from 23 'Extreme Fear' yesterday -- sentiment improving modestly but still fearful, no inflection to greed). NAV 99,837.25 (+0.69% vs July 5), a fifth straight up day for crypto-led gains. Crypto sleeve 19.88% of NAV -- essentially at the 20% neutral cap, no room to add. Cash 13.53% of NAV, comfortably above the 5% floor. No position exceeds the 15% entry cap (MSFT largest at 14.83%) and none breach the ~20% drift-trim trigger. No invalidation levels breached. Net read: equities near highs but showing sector-specific fragility in chips (watch AVGO/semis), rates ticking up on an oil-driven inflation scare (watch for follow-through), crypto grinding higher out of Fear -- mixed signals, insufficient evidence to flip risk-on (hike-odds increase argues against adding beta) or risk-off (index still near record, crypto still bid). REGIME UNCHANGED (neutral). HOLD day, zero trades -- crypto sleeve at cap, all stock positions reasonably sized, no watchlist name clears a compelling entry bar against a backdrop of rising rate-hike odds and chip-sector volatility.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta at $747.71 (above $741.75 avgCost; +0.80%, ~13.13% NAV). Index pulled back 0.45% July 7 on a chip-sector-specific rout, not broad macro weakness; still near record levels reached July 6. Broad index remains the diversified anchor. Hold.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed multi-hike path forcing a technical downtrend in the broad index.

NVDA.thesis: AI/compute secular leader at $196.93 (below $208.44 avgCost; -5.52%, ~13.72% NAV). Closed +0.71% July 7 even as the broader semiconductor group sold off >4.5% on Samsung earnings and DeepSeek in-house-chip reports -- relative strength notable, but sector fragility bears watching (see AVGO on watchlist, down 0.83% same day). AI capex/data-center demand narrative intact. HOLD.
NVDA.invalidationLevel: AI capex digestion or data-center demand rollover; close below 150 USD.

MSFT.invalidationLevel: Cloud growth deceleration or AI capex without demonstrable ROI; close below 340 USD.

GOOGL.invalidationLevel: Search share erosion to AI rivals causing revenue decline; close below 310 USD.

UNH.invalidationLevel: Further regulatory or medical-cost shock; close below 360 USD.

GLD.invalidationLevel: Real yields spike with strong USD reversal sustained; close below 350 USD.

Crypto sleeve at 19.88% -- essentially at the 20% neutral cap, cannot add. HOLD; await regime improvement or sleeve room.
